hypothesis: Augmenting the multimodal contrastive objective with stereochemistry-annotated SMILES and treating mirror-reflected 3D electron densities of each molecule as explicit hard negatives will yield embeddings that remain SO(3)-rotation invariant yet enantiomer-discriminative, achieving at least 75% Top-10 enantiomer retrieval accuracy on a held-out chiral benchmark while keeping rotated-pose Accuracy@10 within 1 percentage point of the original model.
